session_title: The Car — From Nose to Tail

questions.question: How many major systems make up a modern vehicle according to the course framework?
questions.explanation: A modern vehicle is composed of 8 major systems: engine, transmission, electrical/electronics, suspension, steering, brakes, HVAC, and body/interior. Six is too few and would omit critical systems like HVAC and steering.

questions.question: How many characters are contained in a Vehicle Identification Number (VIN)?
questions.explanation: A VIN is a unique 17-character code assigned to every vehicle. Confusing it with 13 characters may stem from older serial number systems that predated the standardised 17-character VIN format.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the differential in a vehicle's transmission system?

choices.A: It converts rotary motion into linear motion
choices.B: It provides multiple gear ratios for different speeds
choices.C: It connects and disconnects the engine from the gearbox
choices.D: It splits drive power between the left and right wheels

questions.explanation: The differential splits power between the left and right wheels, allowing them to rotate at different speeds during turns. Option C describes the clutch or torque converter, not the differential.

questions.question: Which term best describes the shift from purely mechanical vehicles to vehicles controlled by electronic systems and software?
questions.explanation: The mechatronic shift describes the evolution from mechanical components to mechanical parts controlled by electronics and software. 'Electrification upgrade' specifically refers to powertrain electrification (hybrids/EVs), which is only one aspect of the broader mechatronic trend.

questions.question: Positions 1 through 3 of a VIN represent which section?
questions.explanation: Positions 1-3 are the World Manufacturer Identifier (WMI), which encodes the country of origin and manufacturer. The Vehicle Descriptor Section (VDS) occupies positions 4-8 and describes the model, body type, and engine type.

questions.question: A technician is looking up a replacement brake caliper for a customer's vehicle. Two vehicles of the same model and year are in the workshop, but the parts catalogue shows different caliper part numbers for each. What is the most likely reason for this difference?

choices.A: Brake calipers are universal across all trim levels
choices.B: The manufacturer changed suppliers mid-production but the parts are interchangeable
choices.C: One vehicle has a sport package option that uses a larger caliper
choices.D: The parts catalogue contains an error

questions.explanation: Options and trim levels (such as a sport package) frequently change component specifications including brake caliper size. This is precisely why technicians must enter the VIN into the electronic parts catalogue rather than relying on model name alone. The assumption that parts are universal across trim levels is a common and costly mistake.

questions.question: A customer reports that their vehicle pulls to one side during braking and the front tyres show uneven wear on the inner edges. Which combination of vehicle systems is most likely involved?
questions.explanation: Pulling during braking points to a braking system fault (e.g., sticking caliper), while uneven inner-edge tyre wear indicates a suspension geometry issue (e.g., negative camber). This illustrates how multiple systems intersect at the wheel hub area, which was a key observation during the vehicle walkthrough.

questions.question: A technician finds that the VIN stamped on the engine bay of a used vehicle does not match the VIN on the driver's door jamb sticker. What should the technician suspect?

choices.A: A normal manufacturing variation between production stations
choices.B: Possible accident repair with parts from a different vehicle, clocking, or theft
choices.C: The door jamb sticker was replaced during a recall
choices.D: European and North American VIN standards differ and allow two VINs

questions.explanation: All VIN locations on a vehicle must match. A mismatch is a red flag indicating possible major accident repair using panels from another vehicle, odometer fraud (clocking), or a stolen vehicle. VIN standards do not allow different VINs at different locations on the same vehicle.

questions.question: A modern vehicle has over 100 ECUs communicating via a network. Which network protocol enables these ECUs to exchange data with each other?
questions.explanation: CAN bus (Controller Area Network) is the standard in-vehicle communication network over which ECUs exchange data. Bluetooth and Wi-Fi are wireless protocols used for external device connectivity, not for real-time internal ECU communication.

questions.question: During a live vehicle walkthrough, a trainee removes a front wheel and observes a rubber boot on the driveshaft, a brake caliper, a lower control arm, and a tie rod end all in the same area. This observation best demonstrates which key automotive concept?

choices.A: That front-wheel-drive vehicles are more complex than rear-wheel-drive
choices.B: That all components in this area share the same service interval
choices.C: That multiple vehicle systems intersect at a single location, requiring broad system knowledge
choices.D: That the front axle contains more components than the rear axle

questions.explanation: The wheel hub area is where transmission (CV boot), braking (caliper), suspension (control arm), and steering (tie rod) systems all converge. This demonstrates why technicians must understand all vehicle systems rather than specialising in just one. The presence of multiple system components does not imply they share service intervals.
questions.topic: Vehicle walkthrough — systems intersection at wheel hub
